title: Java Package Import
learningGoals: Place the package declaration before imports.
Import a collection type used in a class.

choices: Compilation fails because List is not imported
completionChecks.explanation: List.of("Ana", "Bo") creates a two-element list, so names.size() is 2; the import java.util.List makes List available.

completionChecks.prompt: Where must the package declaration appear?
choices: Before the import declarations, as the first declaration
completionChecks.explanation: A package declaration, when present, must be the first declaration in the source file, before any imports.

completionChecks.prompt: Does this compile if import java.util.List; is placed before package training.app;?
choices: Yes, imports may appear before package declarations
Yes, because java.util is part of the standard library
No, the package declaration must come before imports and class declarations
completionChecks.explanation: A package declaration, when present, must be the first non-comment declaration in the source file, so imports cannot precede it.
